decision.closure_justification_summary: ESIPT is supported by torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry. TICT is supported by torsion-enabled brightening of S1 at 90° and a changed dominant-transition pattern. The missing discriminator is CT localization/charge separation; next step is to quantify charge redistribution (0° vs 90°) from Mulliken charges as a CT surrogate.
decision.pairwise_task_rationale: We have strong ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening consistent with TICT competitiveness, but still lack a decisive CT/charge-separation surrogate tying the 90° brightened state to twisted CT character (and lack direct ES proton-transfer evidence).

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple strong intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds are short/linear and remain essentially invariant across 0–90° torsion, supporting robust ESIPT preconditions; no direct excited-state proton-transfer/tautomer evidence yet.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Torsion snapshots show the lowest state becomes moderately bright at 90° (f≈0.055) with a changed dominant-transition pattern, consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive channel; CT-localization is still not directly established.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A scaffold supports ICT plausibility, but current discriminative evidence is concentrated on ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; no decisive planar ICT signature dominates across torsion.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Heteroatom-rich D–A design plus strong H-bond motifs makes a purely locally excited aromatic mechanism unlikely as primary.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty due to lack of direct ESIPT proton-transfer confirmation and lack of definitive CT-localization metrics for the 90° brightened state.

planner_normalized_response.evidence_summary: Latest microscopic report provides per-snapshot numeric S0 Mulliken charges (110 values) and S0 dipoles for torsion snapshots 0/30/60/90°, and state-1 vertical excitation energy/oscillator strength plus dominant transitions. Key contrast: 0° S1 is dark (f=0.0014; transitions mainly 204→207/208 and 202→207/208), while 90° S1 is much brighter (f=0.0552; transitions mainly into virtual 205). S0 dipole increases from 6.873 D (0°) to 8.011 D (90°). CT-localization proxy remains unavailable.
planner_normalized_response.main_gap: Decisively discriminate ESIPT vs TICT by establishing whether the torsion-brightened 90° S1 is associated with increased charge separation/redistribution consistent with CT (supporting TICT) versus not (leaving ESIPT as dominant).
planner_normalized_response.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT remains top because strong ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bonds persist across torsion and thus ESIPT remains feasible even in twisted geometries. TICT remains the main challenger because twisting to 90° strongly brightens S1 and changes the dominant-transition pattern; however, without a decisive CT-localization/charge-separation surrogate for that brightened state (and without direct ES proton transfer evidence), the pair remains unresolved.
planner_normalized_response.capability_assessment: Microscopic can generate torsion snapshots and provides per-snapshot vertical energies/oscillator strengths, S0 dipoles, S0 Mulliken charges, and dominant transitions. It cannot perform excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer coordinate scans or provide definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation, CT fraction). Verifier retrieval remains non-functional (provider/model-id error), so closure must rely on internal surrogates derivable from existing artifacts.
planner_normalized_response.stagnation_assessment: Mild risk of stagnation if we keep requesting unavailable CT-localization descriptors; however, we now have actual numeric S0 Mulliken vectors and dipoles available per snapshot, so a derived charge-redistribution surrogate is still an actionable, non-redundant discriminator path.
planner_normalized_response.contraction_reason: Verifier supplementation is blocked by provider/model-id error. Several higher-level CT-localization descriptor families are not stored in the available artifacts, forcing reliance on indirect surrogates (dominant transitions, dipole, Mulliken charge redistribution).
planner_normalized_response.information_gain_assessment: Medium: the system now has sufficient numeric S0 charge/dipole data across torsion to build a quantitative CT surrogate, but it still lacks direct state-resolved CT localization and direct ESIPT proton-transfer confirmation.
planner_normalized_response.gap_trend: Narrowing: ESIPT preconditions are robustly established; remaining uncertainty is increasingly concentrated on electronic/charge-separation character of the torsion-brightened state.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Upheld/slightly strengthened because torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry remains the most specific positive evidence and is compatible with emission even when twisted.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Upheld as strong runner-up because 90° torsion strongly brightens S1 and changes the dominant-transition pattern, consistent with torsion-enabled emissive channels; still needs CT/charge-separation confirmation.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Downweighted because the decisive competition is ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; no unique evidence favors planar ICT dominance.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.neutral aromatic: Held low because D–A and H-bond motifs plus torsion-enabled reordering argue against a simple LE aromatic mechanism.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Held low because uncertainty is specific (CT nature of the 90° brightened state; direct ES proton transfer evidence) rather than unconstrained.

planner_normalized_response.verifier_supplement_summary: Verifier retrieval remains unusable due to provider/model-id error; no external evidence cards are available.
planner_normalized_response.closure_justification_summary: ESIPT is supported by torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry. TICT is supported by torsion-enabled brightening of S1 at 90° and a changed dominant-transition pattern. The missing discriminator is CT localization/charge separation; next step is to quantify charge redistribution (0° vs 90°) from Mulliken charges as a CT surrogate.
planner_normalized_response.pairwise_task_rationale: We have strong ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening consistent with TICT competitiveness, but still lack a decisive CT/charge-separation surrogate tying the 90° brightened state to twisted CT character (and lack direct ES proton-transfer evidence).

details.reasoning_summary: The requested execution maps to the registry action extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle, which accepts artifact_bundle_id, state_window, and descriptor_scope including 'mulliken_charges' (and can request 'ground_state_dipole'). The action will parse existing artifacts without new calculations and return CT-descriptor records; numeric Mulliken charge vectors are a supported CT-descriptor family when present. The requested delta computations are a local post-processing step applied to extracted Mulliken vectors and are feasible once the per-snapshot numeric arrays are available.
details.capability_limit_note: extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle can only extract descriptors that are actually present in the artifact bundle. If per-snapshot Mulliken charges or numeric ground-state dipoles are not present in the bundle, the action must report those outputs as unavailable rather than inferring them. The worker cannot create new ab initio data here.
expected_outputs: Explicit numeric Mulliken atomic-charge arrays for snapshot torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°) if present in the bundle.
(If available) numeric ground_state_dipole for the same snapshots.

Fragment-summed ΔQ for the split defined by dihedral atoms [0,1,2,3], where fragment A contains atom 0 and fragment B contains atom 3 (ΔQ_A = sum_{i in A} Δq_i; ΔQ_B = sum_{i in B} Δq_i). If any required Mulliken vectors are missing, report which items are unavailable and skip dependent derived quantities.

details.diagnosis: Round 13/20 (8 rounds remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.52) vs Top2=TICT (0.32), with ICT (0.10) a distant third. Latest evidence strengthens ESIPT slightly because we now have concrete, per-snapshot numeric S0 Mulliken charges and S0 dipoles (at least for torsion_01) plus consistent torsion-dependent S1 data, yet we still cannot extract a state-resolved CT-localization metric tying the 90° brightened S1 to true twisted charge transfer. TICT remains the main challenger because the 90° snapshot shows a large oscillator-strength increase for S1 (f≈0.0552 vs ≈0.0014 at 0°) and a qualitatively different dominant-transition pattern (virtual orbital changes from ~207/208 to 205), consistent with torsion-enabled channel involvement. ICT is further deprioritized because the discriminative axis is now ESIPT vs torsion-enabled (TICT-like) behavior, and we lack quantitative CT-localization descriptors supporting planar ICT dominance. Key remaining discriminator ESIPT vs TICT: whether twisting produces genuine charge separation/CT character (supporting TICT) versus merely reorders a non-CT state while ESIPT remains the dominant emissive relaxation. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool error), and closure justification is still collecting; internal evidence is strong on ESIPT preconditions and on torsion-enabled brightening but not decisive on CT localization, so the best next action is a single bounded microscopic parse/inspection to retrieve torsion_04 Mulliken charges (and then compute Δq) as the most accessible CT surrogate.
details.task_instruction: MICROSCOPIC (single registry action; parse-only): run `inspect_raw_artifact_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots' (perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true) and specifically locate whether torsion_04 (90°) contains an S0 Mulliken charge table (110 values) in any of the available files (s0_stdout.log, s0_aop, s0_mo). Return: (1) exact file path(s) and line/section pointers for the torsion_04 Mulliken charge table; (2) if present, extract and return the 110-value Mulliken charge vector for torsion_04; (3) confirm torsion_01 Mulliken charge vector is already available and report whether torsion_04 uses the same atom ordering. Goal: enable Planner-side Δq=q90°−q0° as a CT surrogate to discriminate ESIPT vs TICT. Do not run new calculations or attempt excited-state relaxation.

agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Run exactly one Amesp action: `inspect_raw_artifact_bundle` with artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots', perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true. Focus on snapshot torsion_04 (90°): identify whether an S0 Mulliken charge table exists in s0_stdout_path / s0_aop_path / s0_mo_path; report file path(s) + section pointers and, if possible, extract and return the full 110-value Mulliken charge vector for torsion_04. Also confirm atom ordering consistency vs torsion_01.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is favored because ESIPT-enabling H-bond geometry is strong and torsion-robust. TICT remains the primary challenger because torsion to 90° strongly brightens S1 and alters dominant-transition patterns, but we still lack a decisive CT/charge-separation surrogate demonstrating that the 90° brightened state is truly CT-localized (and we also lack direct ES proton-transfer evidence).
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tools can generate torsion snapshots and parse per-snapshot S1 excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions; ESIPT preconditions (H-bond geometry) are extractable and robust. However, definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation, CT fraction) and excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer coordinate scans are not supported. Verifier retrieval is currently non-functional (provider/model-id error), so closure must rely on internal surrogates (e.g., Mulliken charge redistribution across torsion) extractable from existing artifacts.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Strong, torsion-robust intramolecular O–H···A H-bond geometries persist across 0–90° torsion, supporting ESIPT preconditions; no internal CT-localization metric has displaced this as the most specific positive evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, S1 becomes much brighter (f≈0.055) and the dominant-transition pattern changes (different virtual orbital), consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive channel; still lacks definitive CT localization or excited-state minimum evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Donor–acceptor scaffold supports ICT plausibility, but the key competition is ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior and no stable planar ICT signature or CT-localization descriptor has been obtained.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Less consistent with heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, strong intramolecular H-bonding motifs, and torsion-sensitive reordering/brightening behavior.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty due to lack of direct ES proton transfer/tautomer evidence and lack of definitive CT-localization metrics for the torsion-brightened state.

planner_normalized_response.closure_justification_summary: ESIPT is supported by torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry. TICT is supported by torsion-enabled brightening of S1 at 90° and changed dominant-transition pattern. The missing discriminator remains CT localization/charge separation; next step is to recover torsion_04 numeric Mulliken charges from raw files to compute Δq(90°−0°) as a CT surrogate.
planner_normalized_response.pairwise_task_rationale: We have strong ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening consistent with TICT competitiveness, but still lack a decisive CT/charge-separation surrogate tying the 90° brightened state to twisted CT character (and lack direct ES proton-transfer evidence).

details.main_gap: Decisive ESIPT vs TICT discriminator: quantify charge redistribution/charge separation associated with the torsion-brightened 90° state (or at least torsion-driven S0 charge redistribution as a proxy) by obtaining torsion_04 numeric Mulliken charges to compute Δq=q90°−q0°; without this, CT localization remains unconfirmed.

details.reasoning_summary: The requested operation maps directly to inspect_raw_artifact_bundle in the action registry, which is intended to report raw-file coverage and extractable observables from an existing bundle. The required parameters (perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true, artifact_bundle_id) are supported. I will request inspection focused on S0 Mulliken-related raw paths and atom-ordering checks. Note that raw inspection may find files and permit extraction, but extraction success depends on file content/format.
details.capability_limit_note: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle will enumerate available raw files and report which observable families are extractable from those files, but it does not guarantee chemistry-specific parsing for every nonstandard raw format. If Mulliken charges are embedded in standard stdout/AOP/MO files, extraction is likely; if stored in nonstandard or truncated artifacts, I will report availability but may be unable to return a clean 110-value vector. Atom-ordering comparison is possible if both snapshot files present explicit atom-indexed charge tables or consistent coordinate sections; otherwise only best-effort mapping can be reported.

Latest evidence (inspect_raw_artifact_bundle on round_05_torsion_snapshots) is net-neutral but functionally weakens progress toward closure: although it confirms that per-snapshot raw files exist (s0_stdout/s0_aop/s0_mo etc.) and that 'mulliken_charges' is nominally an extractable observable family, the requested focused deliverables (torsion_04 Mulliken table extraction + atom-ordering consistency vs torsion_01) were not recovered. This leaves the key ESIPT-vs-TICT discriminator unresolved: whether the torsion-brightened 90° lowest state corresponds to a genuinely CT-localized twisted state (supporting TICT dominance) versus a reordered non-CT state while ESIPT remains dominant. Verifier supplementation remains missing due to provider/model-id error; closure justification remains collecting and still relies on internal surrogates.
details.task_instruction: MICROSCOPIC (single registry action; parse-only): run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots' with state_window=[1] and descriptor_scope=['dominant_transitions','oscillator_strengths','vertical_excitation_energies'] only. Return explicit per-snapshot numeric values for torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°): (i) excitation_energy_ev(S1), (ii) oscillator_strength(S1), and (iii) dominant_transitions list for S1. Goal: if Mulliken charges remain inaccessible, use a transition-pattern-based CT surrogate (orbital participation changes) as the best remaining internal discriminator for ESIPT vs TICT without new calculations.

agent_task_instructions.microscopic: Run exactly one Amesp action: `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` with artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots', perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true, state_window=[1], descriptor_scope=['dominant_transitions','oscillator_strengths','vertical_excitation_energies']. Ensure the response includes actual numeric per-snapshot values and the dominant-transition lists for torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°).

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT remains favored due to torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond preconditions, but TICT remains a strong challenger because twisting to 90° substantially brightens the lowest state and changes dominant-transition patterns. The key missing discriminator is CT localization/charge separation for the 90° brightened state; direct ES proton-transfer evidence is also unavailable.
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic can robustly generate torsion snapshots and parse excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions, and it can parse H-bond geometry. However, attempts to extract numeric Mulliken charge tables for specific snapshots (needed for Δq CT surrogates) have repeatedly failed/been reported missing. Verifier is currently non-functional due to provider/model-id error, so closure must rely on internal surrogates present in artifacts (dominant transitions + f + energy trends).

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Strong ESIPT preconditions remain the most specific positive evidence: short/linear intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds persist essentially unchanged across 0–90° torsion. Latest raw-bundle inspection still cannot extract the missing torsion_04 Mulliken table to support a competing CT-localization surrogate.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Torsion-enabled brightening at 90° (low-lying state becomes moderately bright) and changed dominant-transition pattern support a torsion-enabled emissive channel. However, repeated inability to recover CT/charge-separation surrogates for the 90° snapshot prevents confirmation that this brightened state is truly CT-localized (i.e., TICT rather than a reordered non-CT state).

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Donor–acceptor scaffold supports ICT plausibility, but current discriminative conflict is ESIPT vs TICT. No stable planar ICT signature or quantitative CT-localization metric has been obtained.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Unlikely primary mechanism given heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, strong intramolecular H-bond motifs, and torsion-sensitive state reordering/brightening.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty remains because neither direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence nor definitive CT-localization/charge-separation evidence for the 90° brightened channel is currently available.

details.diagnosis: Round 15/20 (6 remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.55) vs Top2=TICT (0.29), ICT (0.09) third. Latest evidence strengthens the existing internal closure picture: we now have per-snapshot, explicit S1 energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions for torsion_01–torsion_04. At 90° (torsion_04), S1 is strongly brightened (f≈0.0552) and the dominant transitions switch to a different virtual orbital (virtual 205 vs 207/208 at smaller torsion), supporting TICT competitiveness. However, ESIPT remains favored because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bonds were previously shown to be strong and essentially invariant across torsion, and CT-localization proxies remain unavailable (ct_localization_proxy='not_available'), so the key discriminator (is the 90° brightened state truly CT-localized?) is still unresolved. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool failure), and closure justification is still collecting (internal-only) because we lack a decisive CT-localization or direct proton-transfer signature. Best next action is a single microscopic parse that forces retrieval of per-snapshot S0 Mulliken charge arrays (already indicated present via mulliken_charge_count=110) for 0° vs 90°, enabling a derived Δq CT-surrogate at the Planner level without new calculations.
details.task_instruction: MICROSCOPIC (single registry action; parse-only): run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots' with perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true, state_window=[] (or omit), and descriptor_scope=['mulliken_charges']. REQUIRE returning the actual numeric Mulliken charge arrays per snapshot (length 110) at least for torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°), not just counts/availability. If the tool supports snapshot filtering, restrict output to those two snapshots. Goal: compute Δq = q(90°) − q(0°) as an internal CT/charge-redistribution surrogate to discriminate ESIPT vs TICT.

Return the full numeric Mulliken charge arrays (110 values) per snapshot; explicitly include torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°) arrays in the response payload.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT leads due to strong torsion-robust H-bond preconditions, but TICT remains a strong challenger because 90° torsion produces a much brighter S1 with a different dominant-transition pattern. The remaining discriminator is whether this torsion-brightened state corresponds to charge separation/redistribution consistent with a twisted CT state (TICT) versus a reordered non-CT state while ESIPT dominates.

It cannot provide definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation/CT fraction) or excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer scans. Verifier retrieval remains non-functional (provider/model-id error).

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Strong, torsion-robust intramolecular O–H···A hydrogen-bond geometry persists across 0–90° torsion, supporting ESIPT preconditions; no decisive CT-localization evidence has been obtained to overrule this.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, S1 becomes much brighter (f≈0.055) and dominant transitions change to a different virtual orbital, consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive channel; still lacks explicit CT-localization proof and excited-state relaxed minimum evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A scaffold could support ICT, but current discriminative evidence centers on ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; no stable planar ICT signature or definitive CT metrics are available.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Less consistent with heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, multiple strong H-bond motifs, and torsion-dependent state reordering/brightening.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty due to lack of direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer confirmation and lack of definitive CT-localization metrics for the torsion-brightened channel.

decision.diagnosis: Round 15/20 (6 remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.55) vs Top2=TICT (0.29), ICT (0.09) third. Latest evidence strengthens the existing internal closure picture: we now have per-snapshot, explicit S1 energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions for torsion_01–torsion_04. At 90° (torsion_04), S1 is strongly brightened (f≈0.0552) and the dominant transitions switch to a different virtual orbital (virtual 205 vs 207/208 at smaller torsion), supporting TICT competitiveness. However, ESIPT remains favored because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bonds were previously shown to be strong and essentially invariant across torsion, and CT-localization proxies remain unavailable (ct_localization_proxy='not_available'), so the key discriminator (is the 90° brightened state truly CT-localized?) is still unresolved. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool failure), and closure justification is still collecting (internal-only) because we lack a decisive CT-localization or direct proton-transfer signature. Best next action is a single microscopic parse that forces retrieval of per-snapshot S0 Mulliken charge arrays (already indicated present via mulliken_charge_count=110) for 0° vs 90°, enabling a derived Δq CT-surrogate at the Planner level without new calculations.
decision.task_instruction: MICROSCOPIC (single registry action; parse-only): run `extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots' with perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true, state_window=[] (or omit), and descriptor_scope=['mulliken_charges']. REQUIRE returning the actual numeric Mulliken charge arrays per snapshot (length 110) at least for torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°), not just counts/availability. If the tool supports snapshot filtering, restrict output to those two snapshots. Goal: compute Δq = q(90°) − q(0°) as an internal CT/charge-redistribution surrogate to discriminate ESIPT vs TICT.

details.diagnosis: Round 16/20 (5 remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.56), Top2=TICT (0.28). Latest microscopic parsing confirms robust per-snapshot electronic outputs and shows strong torsion-enabled brightening at 90° (S1 f≈0.0552 at 3.385 eV vs 0° S1 f≈0.0014 at 3.3965 eV) with a different dominant-transition pattern at 90°. However, the key ESIPT evidence (torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry) remains unchallenged, and the attempted CT-surrogate extraction remains internally inconsistent at the summary level (route_summary flags mulliken_charges as missing) despite structured snapshot records containing full S0 Mulliken arrays (e.g., torsion_01). This keeps ESIPT favored while leaving TICT as the main challenger. The remaining discriminator is whether the 90° brightened state is genuinely charge-transfer localized (supporting TICT) versus a reordered/non-CT state while ESIPT dominates. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool failure), and closure justification is still collecting; the best next action is to obtain a reliable CT/charge-separation surrogate using an internal, low-cost parse-only action that directly inspects raw S0/S1 stdout/aop for torsion_04 and extracts the Mulliken table (or confirms definitively it is absent) so Δq(90°−0°) can be computed.
details.task_instruction: MICROSCOPIC (single registry action; parse-only): run `inspect_raw_artifact_bundle` on artifact_bundle_id='round_05_torsion_snapshots' (perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true). Focus specifically on snapshot torsion_04 (90°): (1) locate the S0 Mulliken charge table in the raw files (s0_stdout_path and/or s0_aop_path and/or s0_mo_path), (2) extract and return the full 110-value Mulliken charge vector for torsion_04 if present, and (3) explicitly confirm whether atom ordering matches torsion_01. If torsion_04 Mulliken table is not present, return a definitive pointer-based explanation (which files were checked and which sections were absent). Goal: enable Planner-side Δq=q(90°)−q(0°) as a CT surrogate for ESIPT vs TICT.

Identify exact file path(s) and section markers for any S0 Mulliken charge table; extract and return the full 110-value vector if present; confirm atom-order consistency vs torsion_01.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is favored by strong, torsion-robust intramolecular H-bond preconditions. TICT remains a strong challenger due to torsion-enabled brightening and changed dominant-transition patterns at 90°. The key uncertainty is CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; current tooling provides no definitive CT-localization metric and the Mulliken-table availability is inconsistent across summary vs per-snapshot records.
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tooling can generate torsion snapshots and reliably parse vertical excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions; it can also parse intramolecular H-bond geometry. Definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation/CT fraction) and excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer scans are not supported. Verifier retrieval remains non-functional (provider/model-id error), so closure must rely on internal surrogates derivable from raw artifacts (e.g., Mulliken charge redistribution and transition-pattern changes).

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple short/near-linear intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds persist essentially unchanged across 0–90° torsion snapshots, indicating robust ESIPT preconditions; no definitive CT-localization evidence has been recovered for the torsion-brightened state.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, the lowest vertical state becomes much brighter (f≈0.055) and dominant transition patterns differ versus 0°, consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive pathway; still lacks confirmed CT/charge-separation character and relaxed twisted minimum evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A scaffold and conjugation could support ICT, but current discriminative evidence concentrates on ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; CT-localization metrics remain unavailable.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Less consistent with heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, strong H-bond motifs, and torsion-dependent reordering/brightening behavior.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual bucket reflecting missing decisive observables: direct proton-transfer/tautomer evidence and definitive CT-localization for the 90° brightened state remain unavailable.

decision.verifier_supplement_summary: Verifier retrieval remains unusable due to provider/model-id error; no external evidence cards are available.
decision.closure_justification_summary: ESIPT is supported by torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry. TICT is supported by torsion-enabled brightening at 90° (S1 f≈0.055) and changed dominant-transition pattern. The remaining discriminator is CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; next step is targeted raw-bundle inspection to extract/confirm torsion_04 Mulliken charges for a Δq surrogate.
decision.pairwise_task_rationale: Robust ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening both exist; CT/charge-separation evidence for the 90° brightened state (needed to confirm TICT) is still missing/inconsistent, and direct ES proton-transfer evidence is unavailable.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple short/near-linear intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds persist essentially unchanged across 0–90° torsion snapshots, indicating robust ESIPT preconditions; no definitive CT-localization evidence has been recovered for the torsion-brightened state.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, the lowest vertical state becomes much brighter (f≈0.055) and dominant transition patterns differ versus 0°, consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive pathway; still lacks confirmed CT/charge-separation character and relaxed twisted minimum evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A scaffold and conjugation could support ICT, but current discriminative evidence concentrates on ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; CT-localization metrics remain unavailable.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Less consistent with heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, strong H-bond motifs, and torsion-dependent reordering/brightening behavior.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual bucket reflecting missing decisive observables: direct proton-transfer/tautomer evidence and definitive CT-localization for the 90° brightened state remain unavailable.

planner_normalized_response.evidence_summary: Latest microscopic report provides per-snapshot vertical-state details: torsion_01 S1 at 3.3965 eV with f=0.0014; torsion_04 S1 at 3.3850 eV with f=0.0552; dominant-transition pattern differs substantially between 0° and 90°. ESIPT-supporting H-bond robustness across torsion remains previously established. Attempts to extract Mulliken charges for CT surrogate remain inconsistent at the summary layer despite per-snapshot records indicating mulliken_charge_count=110.
planner_normalized_response.main_gap: Decisively separate ESIPT vs TICT by establishing a CT/charge-separation surrogate for the torsion-brightened 90° state (e.g., Δq from Mulliken charges between 0° and 90° and/or other state/fragment charge-shift indicators).
planner_normalized_response.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is favored by strong, torsion-robust intramolecular H-bond preconditions. TICT remains a strong challenger due to torsion-enabled brightening and changed dominant-transition patterns at 90°. The key uncertainty is CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; current tooling provides no definitive CT-localization metric and the Mulliken-table availability is inconsistent across summary vs per-snapshot records.
planner_normalized_response.capability_assessment: Microscopic tooling can generate torsion snapshots and reliably parse vertical excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions; it can also parse intramolecular H-bond geometry. Definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation/CT fraction) and excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer scans are not supported. Verifier retrieval remains non-functional (provider/model-id error), so closure must rely on internal surrogates derivable from raw artifacts (e.g., Mulliken charge redistribution and transition-pattern changes).
planner_normalized_response.stagnation_assessment: Mild stagnation risk: repeated attempts to obtain a decisive CT surrogate have been hampered by inconsistent surfacing of Mulliken numeric tables (present in some structured snapshot records but flagged as missing in route summaries). The next step directly targets raw-file confirmation/extraction for torsion_04 to break this bottleneck.
planner_normalized_response.contraction_reason: External verifier is blocked (invalid provider/model-id). Advanced CT-localization descriptors are not present. Additionally, bundle parsing sometimes returns availability metadata without reliably surfacing numeric Mulliken tables for all snapshots, forcing raw-file inspection.
planner_normalized_response.information_gain_assessment: Medium expected: confirming/extracting torsion_04 Mulliken tables enables a quantitative Δq surrogate; if tables are definitively absent, it justifies switching to alternative internal discriminators (transition-pattern-only) as best-available closure.
planner_normalized_response.gap_trend: Narrowing slowly: ESIPT preconditions are strong and stable; remaining uncertainty is concentrated on CT character of the torsion-brightened state, currently bottlenecked by inconsistent Mulliken table access.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Upweight slightly: torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry is the most specific positive evidence and remains intact; CT-localization evidence for TICT is still not demonstrated.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Hold slightly down: torsion-enabled brightening and transition-pattern changes at 90° support competitiveness, but lack of confirmed CT/charge-separation keeps it below ESIPT.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Hold low: not the active top2 conflict and lacks discriminative CT metrics within current artifacts.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Hold low: uncertainty is targeted (CT surrogate and proton-transfer confirmation), not unstructured.

details.capability_limit_note: inspect_raw_artifact_bundle can enumerate available raw files and report which observable families appear extractable, and may surface file paths and section markers. details.diagnosis: Round 18/20 (3 remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.60) vs Top2=TICT (0.26). Latest evidence (parse_snapshot_outputs on round_05_torsion_snapshots) strengthens ESIPT overall by reaffirming ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bond geometry can remain robust under torsion, while also preserving a meaningful TICT challenge: at 90° (torsion_04) the lowest state is bright (S1: 3.385 eV, f=0.0552; lowest_state_is_bright), whereas at 0° (torsion_01) the lowest state is very dark (S1: 3.3965 eV, f=0.0014; no_bright_state_detected with max f=0.0412 appearing at higher states). The dominant-transition pattern changes substantially between 0° and 90° (e.g., torsion_01 S1 dominated by 204→207/208, while torsion_04 S1 is dominated by 204/203/200/196→205), consistent with torsion-enabled electronic reorganization. However, the key discriminator remains unresolved: whether the 90° brightened lowest state is genuinely CT-localized (supporting TICT as dominant) versus a non-CT reordered state while ESIPT still dominates emission. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool/provider error). Closure justification for ESIPT vs TICT remains collecting; we have strong precondition evidence for ESIPT and strong torsion-enabled brightening evidence for TICT, but lack CT localization/proton-transfer confirmation. With only 3 rounds left, the best next step is a single additional microscopic parse/extraction attempt targeted at any CT surrogate that is actually accessible from stored artifacts to directly test TICT vs ESIPT.

Return per-snapshot numeric values for torsion_01 (0°) and torsion_04 (90°). If numeric Mulliken vectors are unavailable, still return numeric S0 dipole vectors/magnitudes for torsion_01 vs torsion_04. Goal: obtain any quantitative charge/dipole shift proxy between 0° and 90° to support or weaken the TICT (CT-like) interpretation versus ESIPT.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is favored because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bond geometry is strong and torsion-robust. TICT remains the main challenger because twisting to 90° makes the lowest state significantly brighter and changes dominant transitions. The unresolved discriminator is whether that 90° brightened lowest state is CT-like (TICT) versus a reordered non-CT state while ESIPT remains the dominant emissive relaxation pathway; direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence is also not available.
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tooling can robustly parse torsion-snapshot vertical excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions, and can extract H-bond geometry. However, it cannot perform excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer scans and has repeatedly failed to expose definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation/CT fraction) or reliably return snapshot-specific numeric Mulliken tables; verifier retrieval is also non-functional due to provider/model-id error.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Strong ESIPT preconditions persist across torsion: intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds remain short/near-linear and essentially invariant from 0° to 90°. This torsion-robust H-bond network supports ESIPT feasibility even in twisted conformations.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Torsion to 90° produces a much brighter lowest vertical state (S1 f≈0.0552 at 3.385 eV) relative to 0° (S1 f≈0.0014 at 3.3965 eV), with clear changes in dominant-transition patterns, consistent with a torsion-enabled emissive channel. However, CT/charge-separation localization is not available, preventing decisive TICT confirmation.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A architecture could support ICT, but current discriminative evidence concentrates on ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; CT-localization metrics and stable planar ICT signatures are not established.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Given heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold and strong intramolecular H-bond motifs, a purely locally excited aromatic mechanism is unlikely as the primary driver.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty remains because direct ESIPT proton-transfer/tautomer evidence and definitive CT-localization/charge-separation diagnostics for the 90° bright state are not available within current bounded tooling.

decision.diagnosis: Round 18/20 (3 remaining incl. current). Top1=ESIPT (0.60) vs Top2=TICT (0.26). Latest evidence (parse_snapshot_outputs on round_05_torsion_snapshots) strengthens ESIPT overall by reaffirming ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bond geometry can remain robust under torsion, while also preserving a meaningful TICT challenge: at 90° (torsion_04) the lowest state is bright (S1: 3.385 eV, f=0.0552; lowest_state_is_bright), whereas at 0° (torsion_01) the lowest state is very dark (S1: 3.3965 eV, f=0.0014; no_bright_state_detected with max f=0.0412 appearing at higher states). The dominant-transition pattern changes substantially between 0° and 90° (e.g., torsion_01 S1 dominated by 204→207/208, while torsion_04 S1 is dominated by 204/203/200/196→205), consistent with torsion-enabled electronic reorganization. However, the key discriminator remains unresolved: whether the 90° brightened lowest state is genuinely CT-localized (supporting TICT as dominant) versus a non-CT reordered state while ESIPT still dominates emission. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool/provider error). Closure justification for ESIPT vs TICT remains collecting; we have strong precondition evidence for ESIPT and strong torsion-enabled brightening evidence for TICT, but lack CT localization/proton-transfer confirmation. With only 3 rounds left, the best next step is a single additional microscopic parse/extraction attempt targeted at any CT surrogate that is actually accessible from stored artifacts to directly test TICT vs ESIPT.

details.reasoning_summary: The requested operation maps directly to the registry action extract_ct_descriptors_from_bundle, which supports perform_new_calculation=false, reuse_existing_artifacts_only=true, artifact_bundle_id, and descriptor_scope. details.capability_limit_note: This parse-only action cannot generate new calculations; it can only return descriptors actually present in the stored artifacts. The worker will not infer or fabricate numeric values from other proxies.

details.diagnosis: Round budget: current_round_index=19 of max_rounds=20; rounds_remaining_including_current=2. Top1=ESIPT (0.63) vs Top2=TICT (0.25). Latest evidence keeps ESIPT strengthened: torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry remains the most specific positive evidence for ESIPT feasibility even when twisted. Latest evidence also keeps TICT viable: parse_snapshot_outputs shows strong torsion-enabled brightening at 90° (S1 f≈0.0552) and a major dominant-transition pattern change vs 0°. Key remaining discriminator: whether the 90° brightened S1 is genuinely CT/charge-separated (supporting TICT) versus a reordered non-CT state while ESIPT dominates; current CT-localization proxies remain unavailable. Verifier supplementation is still missing (tool failure), closure justification is still collecting (internal) because the CT-character discriminator is unresolved. With only 1 follow-up round after this, the best next action is one final low-cost internal attempt to extract quantitative dipole/charge proxies (0° vs 90°) from the torsion-snapshot bundle.

details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT remains favored because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bond geometry is strong and torsion-robust. TICT remains the main challenger because twisting to 90° produces a much brighter lowest state and substantially different dominant transitions. The unresolved uncertainty is the CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state and the lack of direct ESIPT proton-transfer/tautomer evidence.
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tools can (i) generate torsion snapshots, (ii) parse per-snapshot energies/oscillator strengths, and (iii) often provide dominant-transition lists. They cannot perform excited-state relaxation/proton-transfer scans or provide definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation/CT fraction). Verifier retrieval is non-functional due to provider/model-id error, so external evidence cannot be used for closure; we must rely on internal surrogates (dipole/charge shifts, state-ordering/brightness trends).

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Multiple strong intramolecular O–H···A H-bonds are short/near-linear and persist essentially unchanged across 0–90° torsion snapshots, supporting robust ESIPT preconditions even in twisted conformations. No CT-localization evidence is available that would clearly overturn this.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, the lowest vertical state becomes much brighter (S1 f≈0.0552 at 3.385 eV vs 0° S1 f≈0.0014 at 3.3965 eV) and dominant-transition patterns change strongly, consistent with torsion-enabled emissive channel(s). However, explicit CT/charge-separation localization remains unavailable.

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: The scaffold is D–A rich, but the current discriminative competition is ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; no planar-ICT-dominant signature or quantitative CT descriptors were obtained.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Less consistent with heteroatom-rich D–A design and strong H-bond motifs; torsion-sensitive state reordering suggests more than a simple LE aromatic mechanism.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty because direct ESIPT proton-transfer/tautomer evidence and definitive CT-localization for the 90° brightened state are not available with current bounded tooling.

Verifier retrieval is non-functional due to provider/model-id error, so external evidence cannot be used for closure; we must rely on internal surrogates (dipole/charge shifts, state-ordering/brightness trends).
planner_normalized_response.stagnation_assessment: Some risk of stagnation exists because multiple prior attempts to retrieve numeric Mulliken/dipole outputs were reported missing at the route-summary level. However, structured snapshot records suggest dipole magnitudes and Mulliken counts exist; one last focused extraction is justified given the remaining round budget.
planner_normalized_response.contraction_reason: External verifier blocked (provider/model-id error) and CT-localization descriptors are not produced by available artifact outputs; numeric charge/dipole extraction has been inconsistent, forcing reliance on indirect surrogates.
planner_normalized_response.information_gain_assessment: Latest parse_snapshot_outputs provided medium information gain about torsion-enabled brightening and transition-pattern changes but did not resolve CT character; the proposed dipole/charge extraction is potentially high leverage if numeric outputs can be surfaced.
planner_normalized_response.gap_trend: Narrowing slowly; remaining uncertainty is concentrated on CT characterization of the 90° brightened state.

hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ESIPT: Upweighted because torsion-robust intramolecular H-bond geometry persists across all tested angles, making ESIPT structurally feasible even when twisted.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.TICT: Held as strong runner-up because 90° torsion produces a much brighter lowest state and different dominant transitions, but CT localization is still not established.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.ICT: Downweighted because the decisive competition is ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior and no quantitative CT descriptors support planar ICT dominance.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.neutral aromatic: Held low; D–A and H-bond motifs plus torsion-sensitive reordering argue against a purely LE aromatic mechanism as primary.
hypothesis_reweight_explanation.unknown: Kept low; uncertainty is specific (missing CT/proton-transfer proof) rather than unstructured.

planner_normalized_response.verifier_supplement_summary: Verifier retrieval remains unusable due to provider/model-id error; no external evidence cards are available.
planner_normalized_response.closure_justification_summary: ESIPT is supported by torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond geometry (preconditions). TICT is supported by torsion-enabled emergence of a bright lowest state at 90° (S1 f≈0.0552) and major dominant-transition pattern change versus 0°. Missing discriminator: CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; final internal attempt targets dipole/charge-shift proxies.
planner_normalized_response.pairwise_task_rationale: Both torsion-robust ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening consistent with a twisted-channel competitor are present; decisive CT/charge-separation confirmation for the 90° brightened state is still missing.

details.main_gap: Decisively discriminate ESIPT vs TICT by establishing CT/charge-separation character for the torsion-brightened 90° S1 (or the best available proxy: 0°→90° dipole/charge shifts).

details.diagnosis: Budget: round 20/20 (last round; no follow-up possible). Top1=ESIPT (0.66) vs Top2=TICT (0.24). Latest evidence does not add new decisive discriminators beyond what is already known: ESIPT is strengthened/maintained by torsion-robust strong intramolecular O–H···A H-bond geometry (preconditions) that persists from 0° to 90°. TICT remains a serious challenger because torsion to 90° induces a much brighter lowest vertical state (S1 f≈0.055) and a major dominant-transition pattern change versus 0°. Key remaining discriminator between ESIPT and TICT is the charge-transfer/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; current artifacts did not yield reliable numeric charge/dipole-shift proxies or explicit CT-localization metrics. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool failure), and closure justification is still collecting/incomplete; with only this round remaining, the best available stance is to proceed toward best-available finalization favoring ESIPT while explicitly stating the unresolved CT-localization gap for the 90° bright state.
details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is the best-supported mechanism because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bonds are strong and remain intact across large torsion changes (robust preconditions). The main residual uncertainty is that torsion to 90° produces a much brighter lowest vertical state with altered transition patterns, keeping TICT plausible; decisive CT-localization/charge-separation confirmation for that 90° brightened state (and direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence) is not available with current bounded tools.
details.final_hypothesis_rationale: Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'ESIPT' ahead of 'TICT'. The main unresolved gap remains: CT/charge-separation character of the 90° torsion-brightened lowest state (needed to confirm true TICT) and/or direct ESIPT proton-transfer/tautomer evidence (needed to decisively confirm ESIPT beyond preconditions).
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tools provided torsion-snapshot vertical excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions, plus robust H-bond geometry parsing. However, definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation / CT fraction) and direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence (excited-state relaxation/coordinate scans) are unavailable. Verifier retrieval is non-functional (provider/model-id error), so external supplementation could not be used. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.

hypothesis_pool.name: ESIPT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Across torsion snapshots (0/30/60/90°), intramolecular O–H···A H-bond geometry remains strong and essentially invariant (robust ESIPT preconditions). No direct CT-localization metric is available to supersede this as the most specific positive evidence.

hypothesis_pool.name: TICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: At 90° torsion, the lowest vertical state becomes much brighter (S1 f≈0.0552 at 3.385 eV vs 0° S1 f≈0.0014 at 3.3965 eV) and dominant-transition patterns change strongly—consistent with torsion-enabled emissive channels. However, the missing discriminator is CT/charge-separation character of this brightened state (no hole–electron separation / CT fraction / reliable charge-shift proxy).

hypothesis_pool.name: ICT
hypothesis_pool.rationale: D–A scaffold supports ICT plausibility, but the decisive competition is ESIPT vs torsion-enabled behavior; quantitative CT-localization descriptors were unavailable, and torsion behavior does not uniquely support planar ICT dominance.

hypothesis_pool.name: neutral aromatic
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Heteroatom-rich D–A scaffold, strong intramolecular H-bonds, and torsion-dependent state reordering/brightening argue against a purely local aromatic (LE) mechanism as primary.

hypothesis_pool.name: unknown
hypothesis_pool.rationale: Residual uncertainty remains because direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence and definitive CT-localization for the 90° brightened state are not available within current bounded tooling.

agent_reports.remaining_local_uncertainty: Microscopic local uncertainty after this Amesp run: this bounded Amesp route 'artifact_parse_only' does not adjudicate the global mechanism. it does not execute full-DFT or heavy excited-state optimization. it only parses existing artifacts and cannot create new microscopic evidence. targeted follow-up remains bounded by current Amesp route availability and resource limits.

details.verifier_supplement_summary: Verifier retrieval unusable due to provider/model-id error; no external evidence cards were available.
details.closure_justification_summary: Internal evidence supports both: ESIPT via torsion-robust strong intramolecular H-bond preconditions; TICT via torsion-enabled emergence of a bright lowest state at 90° with major dominant-transition changes. The missing decisive discriminator (CT/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state) could not be obtained within bounded tools, so closure is best-available in favor of ESIPT.
details.pairwise_task_rationale: Torsion-robust ESIPT preconditions and torsion-enabled brightening both occur; CT-localization evidence for the 90° brightened state (needed to confirm TICT) was not obtainable with current bounded artifact outputs.
details.diagnosis: Budget: round 20/20 (last round; no follow-up possible). Top1=ESIPT (0.66) vs Top2=TICT (0.24). Latest evidence does not add new decisive discriminators beyond what is already known: ESIPT is strengthened/maintained by torsion-robust strong intramolecular O–H···A H-bond geometry (preconditions) that persists from 0° to 90°. TICT remains a serious challenger because torsion to 90° induces a much brighter lowest vertical state (S1 f≈0.055) and a major dominant-transition pattern change versus 0°. Key remaining discriminator between ESIPT and TICT is the charge-transfer/charge-separation character of the 90° brightened state; current artifacts did not yield reliable numeric charge/dipole-shift proxies or explicit CT-localization metrics. Verifier supplementation remains missing (tool failure), and closure justification is still collecting/incomplete; with only this round remaining, the best available stance is to proceed toward best-available finalization favoring ESIPT while explicitly stating the unresolved CT-localization gap for the 90° bright state.
details.hypothesis_uncertainty_note: ESIPT is the best-supported mechanism because ESIPT-enabling intramolecular H-bonds are strong and remain intact across large torsion changes (robust preconditions). The main residual uncertainty is that torsion to 90° produces a much brighter lowest vertical state with altered transition patterns, keeping TICT plausible; decisive CT-localization/charge-separation confirmation for that 90° brightened state (and direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence) is not available with current bounded tools.
details.final_hypothesis_rationale: Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'ESIPT' ahead of 'TICT'. The main unresolved gap remains: CT/charge-separation character of the 90° torsion-brightened lowest state (needed to confirm true TICT) and/or direct ESIPT proton-transfer/tautomer evidence (needed to decisively confirm ESIPT beyond preconditions).
details.capability_assessment: Microscopic tools provided torsion-snapshot vertical excitation energies/oscillator strengths and dominant transitions, plus robust H-bond geometry parsing. However, definitive CT-localization metrics (hole–electron separation / CT fraction) and direct ES proton-transfer/tautomer evidence (excited-state relaxation/coordinate scans) are unavailable. Verifier retrieval is non-functional (provider/model-id error), so external supplementation could not be used. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.
details.stagnation_assessment: Late-stage bottleneck: repeated attempts to extract numeric charge/dipole-shift proxies from the torsion-snapshot bundle did not yield a decisive CT surrogate; remaining discrimination between ESIPT and TICT could not be resolved within available tool outputs and remaining round budget. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.
details.contraction_reason: External verifier unavailable (provider/model-id error) and internal bounded toolchain cannot compute excited-state relaxed minima or definitive CT-localization metrics; numeric charge/dipole proxy extraction from stored torsion-snapshot artifacts was inconsistent/unavailable. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.
